segments.text: And it came to pass in those days, that the Philistines gathered their armies together for warfare, to fight with Israel.
Know thou assuredly, that thou shalt go out with me to battle, thou and thy men.
segments.speaker: Achish

segments.text: Surely thou shalt know what thy servant can do.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Therefore will I make thee keeper of mine head for ever.
segments.speaker: Achish

segments.text: Now Samuel was dead, and all Israel had lamented him, and buried him in Ramah, even in his own city. And Saul had put away those that had familiar spirits, and the wizards, out of the land.
And the Philistines gathered themselves together, and came and pitched in Shunem: and Saul gathered all Israel together, and they pitched in Gilboa.
And when Saul saw the host of the Philistines, he was afraid, and his heart greatly trembled.
And when Saul enquired of the LORD, the LORD answered him not, neither by dreams, nor by Urim, nor by prophets.
Seek me a woman that hath a familiar spirit, that I may go to her, and enquire of her.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Behold, there is a woman that hath a familiar spirit at Endor.
segments.speaker: Saul's servants

segments.text: And Saul disguised himself, and put on other raiment, and he went, and two men with him, and they came to the woman by night:
I pray thee, divine unto me by the familiar spirit, and bring me him up, whom I shall name unto thee.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Behold, thou knowest what Saul hath done, how he hath cut off those that have familiar spirits, and the wizards, out of the land: wherefore then layest thou a snare for my life, to cause me to die?
segments.speaker: The Woman

segments.text: As the LORD liveth, there shall no punishment happen to thee for this thing.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Whom shall I bring up unto thee?
segments.speaker: The Woman

segments.text: Bring me up Samuel.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And when the woman saw Samuel, she cried with a loud voice:
Why hast thou deceived me? for thou art Saul.
segments.speaker: The Woman

segments.text: Be not afraid: for what sawest thou?
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: I saw gods ascending out of the earth.
segments.speaker: The Woman

segments.text: What form is he of?
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: An old man cometh up; and he is covered with a mantle.
segments.speaker: The Woman

segments.text: And Saul perceived that it was Samuel, and he stooped with his face to the ground, and bowed himself.
Why hast thou disquieted me, to bring me up?
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: I am sore distressed; for the Philistines make war against me, and God is departed from me, and answereth me no more, neither by prophets, nor by dreams: therefore I have called thee, that thou mayest make known unto me what I shall do.
segments.speaker: Saul

footnotes: by prophets: Heb. by the hand of prophets
heading: Samuel's Prophecy

segments.text: Wherefore then dost thou ask of me, seeing the LORD is departed from thee, and is become thine enemy?
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And the LORD hath done to him, as he spake by me: for the LORD hath rent the kingdom out of thine hand, and given it to thy neighbour, even to David:
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Because thou obeyedst not the voice of the LORD, nor executedst his fierce wrath upon Amalek, therefore hath the LORD done this thing unto thee this day.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Moreover the LORD will also deliver Israel with thee into the hand of the Philistines: and to morrow shalt thou and thy sons be with me: the LORD also shall deliver the host of Israel into the hand of the Philistines.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Then Saul fell straightway all along on the earth, and was sore afraid, because of the words of Samuel: and there was no strength in him; for he had eaten no bread all the day, nor all the night.

footnotes: fell: Heb. made haste, and fell with the fulness of his stature

segments.text: Behold, thine handmaid hath obeyed thy voice, and I have put my life in my hand, and have hearkened unto thy words which thou spakest unto me.
segments.speaker: The Woman

segments.text: Now therefore, I pray thee, hearken thou also unto the voice of thine handmaid, and let me set a morsel of bread before thee; and eat, that thou mayest have strength, when thou goest on thy way.
segments.speaker: The Woman

segments.text: I will not eat.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: But his servants, together with the woman, compelled him; and he hearkened unto their voice. So he arose from the earth, and sat upon the bed.
And the woman had a fat calf in the house; and she hasted, and killed it, and took flour, and kneaded it, and did bake unleavened bread thereof:
And she brought it before Saul, and before his servants; and they did eat. Then they rose up, and went away that night.
